name: Git Utilities (VS Code extension)
description: Async wrappers around the git CLI used by the VS Code extension package (NOT the Electron workbench app — that has its own git layer in app-exec-git / app-worktrees). Exports: gitRaw (with timeoutMs support), listWorktrees, listBranches (local + remote-only branches as RemoteBranch {name, remoteRef}), addWorktree, removeWorktree, mergedWorktrees, countUncommitted, aheadBehind, findRepoRoot, findRepoKey, plus the Worktree interface. addWorktree takes an explicit AddWorktreeOptions discriminated union: {mode:'checkout'} (existing local branch), {mode:'checkout-remote', remoteRef} (new local branch tracking a remote), {mode:'create', base?} (new branch from an optional base ref, default HEAD). Re-exports buildRepoKey and repoNameFromCommonDir from @code-workbench/mcp-core/repo-key. `removeWorktree` delegates to @code-workbench/git-utils/remove-worktree for the robust removal flow (timeout + retry + force-detach + pending-deletion rescue).
guidelines: gitRaw accepts an optional `timeoutMs` and surfaces timeouts as `Error('Timed out after Nms: git ...')` so the shared removeWorktreeRobust core recognises them and falls back to manual rm.

removeWorktree takes an optional PendingDeletionStore; the extension passes a globalState-backed store from extension.ts (PENDING_DELETIONS_KEY) so Windows-locked directories survive a window reload and are retried on next activation.

All interactive worktree-creation UI (branch picker with local/remote sections, base-branch picker, path prompt, create + assignColorIfUnset, 'Open in New Window' toast) lives in commands/worktreeCreate.ts — reuse promptWorktree / promptNewBranchWorktree / createWorktree / offerOpen / branchSlug instead of re-implementing the flow in a command.
anti_patterns: Don't reintroduce the inline `git worktree remove --force; fs.rm` fallback — it lacks timeout, retry, detach-on-lock, and pending-deletion recovery. Always go through shared-git-utils.
Don't add boolean flags like the old `createBranch` to addWorktree — use the AddWorktreeOptions mode union so intent (checkout vs create vs track-remote) is explicit.
decisions: Worktree-removal robustness lives in @code-workbench/git-utils so the Electron app and the VS Code extension share one implementation — see [[shared-git-utils]].
addWorktree fetches the branch from its remote best-effort before checkout so ahead/behind counts are accurate; a fetch failure (offline) never blocks worktree creation.
